content: When building related domain entities, map the dependency graph first and build bottom-up — each entity must reference only previously-created entities
Always add a correlation_id column to lifecycle entities (work orders, inspections) and propagate it through all child events for end-to-end tracing
After releasing or finalizing an entity (routing, BOM, inspection plan), guard all mutation paths with a status check that returns an error before touching data
When enforcing sequential operations, use a sequence_number + is_required flag — required predecessors must complete first, optional ones can be skipped
Never rely on Axum path parameter name alone to differentiate endpoints — same-shape paths like /{action_key} and /{rule_id} collide; use a sub-path prefix instead
Investigation beads should produce a working prototype (not just a document) when scope allows — this gives subsequent beads a concrete reference implementation
Well-scoped beads (one entity, one concern, complete vertical slice) enable single-commit delivery — split scope until each bead can land in one atomic commit
Even small fixes to proven modules (>=1.0.0) require a PATCH version bump in Cargo.toml and a REVISIONS.md entry in the same commit
